description.en: Simple class used by default by parse_args() to create an object holding attributes and return it. This class is deliberately simple, just an object subclass with a readable string representation. If you prefer to have dict-like view of the attributes, you can use the standard Python idiom, vars(): >>> parser = argparse.ArgumentParser() >>> parser.add_argument('--foo') >>> args = parser.parse_args(['--foo', 'BAR']) >>> vars(args) {'foo': 'BAR'} It may also be useful to have an ArgumentParser ...
